description: QA validation gates for HIPAA-compliant TypeScript medical records code

verificationChecklist.description: Run these checks on every developer submission. Reject if any blocking issue is found.
codeQuality: npx tsc --noEmit --strict -- must compile with zero errors
grep -rn ': any' src/ -- ZERO occurrences (reject if found)
Count lines per function: no function body over 40 lines (reject if found)
Verify src/ has multiple .ts files -- reject single-file monoliths (e.g. all logic in cli.ts)
Verify interfaces/types exist for data structures (e.g. MedicalRecord, ValidationResult)
testQuality: npm test -- 100% of tests must pass
Count test cases: minimum 3 per requirement (happy path, error, edge case) -- reject if fewer
Verify tests import and call validation functions directly -- not only CLI spawn tests
Check for edge cases: empty file, malformed JSON, missing fields, file not found
Verify assertions check specific values (line numbers, messages) -- not just truthy
traceability: Verify ALL src/ and test files contain REQ-HCDP-XXX comments
Verify requirements match implementation (no orphaned REQ comments)

rejectionCriteria.description: REJECT the submission if ANY of these are true. Move former warnings to blocking.
blockingIssues: Code does not compile (npx tsc --noEmit --strict fails)
All logic in a single file (no modular decomposition)
Fewer than 3 test cases per requirement
Tests only spawn CLI process -- no direct function/unit tests
No TypeScript interfaces defined for data structures
Acceptance criteria not met per checklist

projectContext: HIPAA Security Rule (45 CFR 164) compliance MUST be maintained
Medical records CLI -- data integrity is critical
V-model regulatory pipeline -- traceability is mandatory
